name: output-trim
description: PreToolUse(Bash) stdout trimmer — when a simple single Bash command would emit >8000 chars, transparently rewrite via `updatedInput` to pipe stdout through a minimal hexa-native kernel: consecutive-line dedup (collapses repeated lines with a `[×N]` counter) + per-line truncation to 200 chars. Small outputs (<8000 chars) pass verbatim — preserves exact-value semantics (SHAs, counts). Skipped on any shell metacharacter (`| > >> << & ; && $( \``) so compound pipelines, redirects, heredocs, background jobs, command substitutions, and other PreToolUse(Bash) rewrite hooks (pool-route, pr-automerge) compose without conflict. Exit code preserved via `set -o pipefail`. Kernel chosen by benchmark on 120 real Bash-stdout samples ≥8000 chars: this minimal kernel Pareto-dominates the TF-IDF salience + MinHash near-dup paragraph dedup approach of the original `wilson-output-trim` (48% chars saved vs 45% at equal signal-recall and completeness). NO opt-out by design (commons @D g30).
